signatures.label: useState<S>(initialState: S | (() => S)): [S, Dispatch<SetStateAction<S>>]
signatures.documentation: Returns a stateful value and a function to update it. During the initial render, the returned state is the same as the value passed as the first argument. The setState function accepts a new state value or a function that receives the previous state.

parameters.label: initialState: S | (() => S)
parameters.documentation: The value used for the initial state. Can also be a function that returns the initial state (lazy initialization).

signatures.label: useEffect(effect: EffectCallback, deps?: DependencyList): void
signatures.documentation: Accepts a function that contains imperative, possibly effectful code. Runs the effect after every completed render unless a dependency array is provided.

parameters.label: effect: EffectCallback
parameters.documentation: The effect function to run. May return a cleanup function.

parameters.label: deps?: DependencyList
parameters.documentation: Optional array of dependencies. Effect re-runs only when dependencies change.

signatures.label: useContext<T>(context: Context<T>): T
signatures.documentation: Accepts a context object (the value returned from React.createContext) and returns the current context value. The current context value is determined by the nearest Provider above.

parameters.label: context: Context<T>
parameters.documentation: Context object created by React.createContext.

signatures.label: useReducer<R>(reducer: R, initialState: ReducerState<R>, init?: (initial: ReducerState<R>) => ReducerState<R>): [ReducerState<R>, Dispatch<ReducerAction<R>>]
signatures.documentation: An alternative to useState. Accepts a reducer of type (state, action) => newState, and returns the current state paired with a dispatch method.

parameters.label: reducer: R
parameters.documentation: A function of type (state, action) => newState.

parameters.label: initialState: ReducerState<R>
parameters.documentation: Initial state value for the reducer.

parameters.label: init?: (initial: ReducerState<R>) => ReducerState<R>
parameters.documentation: Optional lazy initializer function.

signatures.label: useMemo<T>(factory: () => T, deps: DependencyList): T
signatures.documentation: Returns a memoized value. Pass a "create" function and an array of dependencies. useMemo will only recompute the memoized value when one of the dependencies has changed.

parameters.label: factory: () => T
parameters.documentation: Function that computes and returns the memoized value.

parameters.label: deps: DependencyList
parameters.documentation: Array of dependencies that trigger recomputation.

signatures.label: useCallback<T extends Function>(callback: T, deps: DependencyList): T
signatures.documentation: Returns a memoized callback. Pass an inline callback and an array of dependencies. useCallback will return a memoized version of the callback that only changes if one of the dependencies has changed.

parameters.label: callback: T
parameters.documentation: The callback function to memoize.

parameters.label: deps: DependencyList
parameters.documentation: Array of dependencies that trigger re-creation of the callback.

signatures.label: useRef<T>(initialValue: T): MutableRefObject<T>
signatures.documentation: Returns a mutable ref object whose .current property is initialized to the passed argument. The returned object will persist for the full lifetime of the component.

parameters.label: initialValue: T
parameters.documentation: Initial value for the ref's .current property. Commonly null for DOM refs.

signatures.label: useImperativeHandle<T, R>(ref: Ref<T>, createHandle: () => R, deps?: DependencyList): void
signatures.documentation: Customizes the instance value that is exposed to parent components when using ref. Should be used with forwardRef.

parameters.label: ref: Ref<T>
parameters.documentation: The ref forwarded from a parent component.

parameters.label: createHandle: () => R
parameters.documentation: Function returning the handle object to expose.

parameters.label: deps?: DependencyList
parameters.documentation: Optional dependency array for recomputing the handle.

signatures.label: useLayoutEffect(effect: EffectCallback, deps?: DependencyList): void
signatures.documentation: The signature is identical to useEffect, but it fires synchronously after all DOM mutations. Use this to read layout from the DOM and synchronously re-render.

parameters.label: effect: EffectCallback
parameters.documentation: Effect function that runs synchronously after DOM mutations.

parameters.label: deps?: DependencyList
parameters.documentation: Optional dependency array.

signatures.label: useDebugValue<T>(value: T, format?: (value: T) => any): void
signatures.documentation: Used to display a label for custom hooks in React DevTools. Optionally accepts a formatting function.

parameters.label: value: T
parameters.documentation: The debug value to display.

parameters.label: format?: (value: T) => any
parameters.documentation: Optional formatting function for expensive computations.

signatures.label: useDeferredValue<T>(value: T): T
signatures.documentation: Accepts a value and returns a deferred version of it that may lag behind the original. Useful for deferring expensive re-renders.

parameters.label: value: T
parameters.documentation: The value to defer.

signatures.label: useSyncExternalStore<T>(subscribe: (onStoreChange: () => void) => () => void, getSnapshot: () => T, getServerSnapshot?: () => T): T
signatures.documentation: Subscribe to an external store. Returns the current snapshot of the store's state.

parameters.label: subscribe: (onStoreChange: () => void) => () => void
parameters.documentation: Function to subscribe to the store. Returns an unsubscribe function.

parameters.label: getSnapshot: () => T
parameters.documentation: Function that returns the current snapshot of the store.

parameters.label: getServerSnapshot?: () => T
parameters.documentation: Optional function returning snapshot for server rendering.

signatures.label: useInsertionEffect(effect: EffectCallback, deps?: DependencyList): void
signatures.documentation: Fires synchronously before all DOM mutations. Used for injecting styles into the DOM before layout effects read them.

parameters.label: effect: EffectCallback
parameters.documentation: Effect for injecting styles or similar pre-layout operations.

parameters.label: deps?: DependencyList
parameters.documentation: Optional dependency array.

signatures.label: createContext<T>(defaultValue: T): Context<T>
signatures.documentation: Creates a Context object. When React renders a component that subscribes to this Context object, it will read the current value from the closest matching Provider.

parameters.label: defaultValue: T
parameters.documentation: The default value used when no Provider is found above in the tree.

signatures.label: createElement(type: string | ComponentType, props?: object, ...children: ReactNode[]): ReactElement
signatures.documentation: Create and return a new React element of the given type. Type can be a tag name string or a React component type.

parameters.label: type: string | ComponentType
parameters.documentation: HTML tag name or React component.

parameters.label: props?: object
parameters.documentation: Props to pass to the element.

parameters.label: ...children: ReactNode[]
parameters.documentation: Child elements.

signatures.label:
memo

(Component: FunctionComponent

, areEqual?: (prev: Readonly

, next: Readonly

) => boolean): NamedExoticComponent
signatures.documentation: If your component renders the same result given the same props, wrap it in React.memo for a performance boost by memoizing the result.

parameters.label: Component: FunctionComponent
parameters.documentation: The component to memoize.

parameters.label:
areEqual?: (prev: Readonly

, next: Readonly

) => boolean
parameters.documentation: Optional custom comparison function. Defaults to shallow comparison.

signatures.label: forwardRef(render: ForwardRefRenderFunction): ForwardRefExoticComponent & RefAttributes>
signatures.documentation: Creates a component that forwards ref to a child. Useful for passing refs to DOM elements or class components inside function components.

parameters.label: render: ForwardRefRenderFunction<T, P>
parameters.documentation: Render function that receives props and ref as arguments.

signatures.label: lazy<T extends ComponentType<any>>(factory: () => Promise<{ default: T }>): LazyExoticComponent<T>
signatures.documentation: Lets you define a component that is loaded dynamically. Helps reduce bundle size by code-splitting components that aren't needed during initial render.

parameters.label: factory: () => Promise<{ default: T }>
parameters.documentation: Function returning a dynamic import() of a module with a default export.

signatures.label: createPortal(children: ReactNode, container: Element, key?: string): ReactPortal
signatures.documentation: Creates a portal. Portals provide a way to render children into a DOM node that exists outside the parent component's DOM hierarchy.

parameters.label: children: ReactNode
parameters.documentation: The React elements to render.

parameters.label: container: Element
parameters.documentation: The DOM element to render into.

parameters.label: key?: string
parameters.documentation: Optional unique key for the portal.

signatures.label: createRoot(container: Element | DocumentFragment, options?: RootOptions): Root
signatures.documentation: Create a React root for a browser DOM element. Use it to display React content inside a browser DOM element. After creating a root, call root.render() to display a React component inside it.

parameters.label: container: Element | DocumentFragment
parameters.documentation: The DOM element to mount the React application into.

parameters.label: options?: RootOptions
parameters.documentation: Optional configuration for the root (identifierPrefix, onRecoverableError, etc.).
